question: What language requirements must I meet to be eligible for appointment at the ECB?
answer: To be eligible for appointment, candidates must have a thorough knowledge of English and a satisfactory knowledge of at least a second official Union language, unless an exception is authorised in accordance with the provisions laid down in the Staff Rules. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 4)

question: What is the maximum duration of a probationary period at the ECB?
answer: The probationary period shall in no circumstances exceed 12 months, and a member of staff shall not be subject to more than one probationary period for the same or an equivalent position. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 6)

question: Is there an income limit on my spouse's earnings that affects my eligibility for the household allowance?
answer: A head of household shall receive the household allowance only when the gross annual income of his or her spouse or recognised partner does not exceed EUR 71,764. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 9)

question: How long does the ECB have to request recovery of a sum that was overpaid to me?
answer: The request for recovery must be made no later than five years from the date on which the sum was overpaid or unduly paid. Where the ECB is able to establish that the recipient deliberately misled the administration in order to obtain the sum, the request for recovery shall not be invalidated even after this period has elapsed. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 11)

question: What installation allowance am I entitled to when I relocate to take up my appointment at the ECB?
answer: Subject to the conditions laid down in the Staff Rules, the ECB grants an installation allowance equal to 200% of the basic monthly salary for members of staff entitled to a household allowance, or 100% of the basic monthly salary in other cases. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 12)

question: How many days of paid annual leave am I entitled to each year?
answer: Members of staff are entitled to thirty and a half working days of paid leave per annum, and public holidays observed by the ECB are additional to this leave entitlement. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 13)

question: What indemnity is paid if a staff member dies as a result of an accident at work?
answer: In the event of death resulting from an occupational disease or accident at work, an indemnity equal to five times the deceased member of staff's annual basic salary shall be paid, in the first instance to the persons known to the ECB and designated by the deceased member of staff. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 16)

question: What unemployment allowance am I entitled to following termination of my contract with the ECB?
answer: Members of staff who are unemployed following termination of their contract with the ECB are entitled to a monthly unemployment allowance equal to 60% of their last basic salary for six months, and 30% of their last basic salary for the subsequent six months and additional months in relation to years of service and age. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 17)

question: If a staff member dies, for how long does the ECB continue to pay their salary and allowances?
answer: In the event of the death of a member of staff, payment of his or her salary and allowances shall be extended for three months. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 19)

question: If I am suspended pending disciplinary proceedings and part of my salary is withheld, when must any withheld amount be reimbursed?
answer: If within nine months from the suspension no final decision has been taken, or no measure other than a written warning or a written reprimand has been adopted, the ECB shall reimburse the member of staff the amount of salary withheld. (ECB Conditions of Employment, page 23)
